channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Ted Cruz and Colin Allred wage another big US Senate fight in Texas
item.description: FORT WORTH, Texas (AP) — Attack ads on every TV break. Campaign money pouring in. And on a sunny Saturday, a crowd stretching out the door for a campaign rally at Tulip's, a popular Fort Worth nightclub — this time for Democratic U.S. Rep.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY and JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fight for House raging in districts ringing NYC where Trump struggled but other Republicans thrived
item.description: Voters in a ring of congressional districts encircling New York City where Republican candidates often do well but Donald Trump struggled in 2020 could decide which party controls the U.S. House for the next two years.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Izaguirre, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Why is Donald Trump campaigning in California, a state he's almost certain to lose?
item.description: The White House election is expected to come down to battlegrounds such as Wisconsin and Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Blood, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: VP Harris releasing detailed medical report, challenging Trump to do the same
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris will release medical records and health information on Saturday in an effort to draw contrast with former President Trump, who is 79.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kremlin sowing chaos with bomb threats and bribery to thwart Moldova's vote to join EU, authorities say
item.description: Buying off voters, calling in bomb threats and paying protesters to antagonize police — these are the tactics authorities say the Kremlin has taken up to thwart an upcoming election in Moldova.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rebuilding Florida after Hurricane Milton and protecting against scams
item.description:
Recovery efforts can unite communities after catastrophic damage. Neighbors come together to help one another, while state officials and law enforcement work diligently to restore order. Gradually, residents begin to see their homes return to normalcy.
But even with the good, comes the bad.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Are male voters reluctant to vote for a woman? Harris' backers are confronting the question head on
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The concern has been there all along, but now it's being talked about openly: Are some men reluctant to vote for Democrat Kamala Harris because she’s a woman? The vice president rarely references her gender on the campaign trail, but her key supporters are starting to make more dir
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden calls VP Harris 'president' at hurricane briefing after being interrupted twice
item.description: President Biden once again referred to Vice President Kamala Harris incorrectly as the "president" during a press briefing on hurricane recovery efforts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Battle to shore up bases remains as Election Day nears
item.description: MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst and his guest talks about deadly conflicts in the Middle East and how those have factored into domestic politics. Plus, we follow up with two Generation Z voters about how they’re approaching the election.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, Matthew Alvarez, and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: JD Vance refused five times to acknowledge Donald Trump lost 2020 election in podcast interview
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — JD Vance, Republican vice presidential nominee, again refused to acknowledge that President Joe Biden won the 2020 election over former President Donald Trump, evading the question five times in an interview with The New York Times, the newspaper reported Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Doug Emhoff doesn't deny report he slapped ex-girlfriend outside overseas movie event
item.description: Second gentleman Doug Emhoff called salacious allegations against him a "distraction" after a Daily Mail report claimed he once struck an ex-girlfriend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris viewed more positively by Hispanic women than by Hispanic men: AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A solid majority of Hispanic women have a positive opinion of Vice President Kamala Harris and a negative view of former President Donald Trump, but Hispanic men are more divided on both candidates, according to a recent poll from The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vladimir Putin meets with Iranian President Pezeshkian to celebrate 'very close' relationship
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin met with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian on Friday, hailing the "very close" relationship between Russia and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why do news media call the winners of elections?
item.description: It’s election night, the polls have closed and chances are you’re waiting on The Associated Press or one of the major television networks to say who will be the next president.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden suggests Trump 'get a life' after false hurricane claims
item.description: “Mr. President Trump, former President Trump, get a life man, help these people,” Biden said in remarks on Hurricane Milton from the White House.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: NJ assemblyman's car stolen, house broken into
item.description: HAZLET, N.J. (PIX11) - Video from the home where a New Jersey assemblyman is currently living shows alleged burglars break into the home and speed away with his personal car - complete with State Assembly plates.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Republican and Democratic House Latinos face off in race to increase their numbers
item.description: Latino House Republicans are building out campaign infrastructure to protect their members and grow their numbers, at times directly competing with their Democratic counterparts, who have a huge head start. Rep.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Candidate Kamilah Bywaters discusses her vision for the Clark County School Board
item.description: Kamilah Bywaters, a candidate for the Clark County School District (CCSD) Board of Trustees, joined 8 News Now’s Daily Voting Guide to discuss her plans and priorities for District E, which covers Summerlin and northwest parts of the Las Vegas Valley.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden postpones trip to Germany and Angola to monitor Hurricane Milton
item.description: President Joe Biden said Tuesday he is postponing a planned trip to Germany and Angola to stay at the White House to monitor Hurricane Milton, as it spins closer to Florida’s Gulf Coast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Candidates for Las Vegas Mayor face off in a debate ahead of Election Day
item.description: Las Vegas Mayoral candidates Shelley Berkley and Victoria Seaman will face off in a debate on Tuesday, hosted by 8 News Now, with the winner taking over the non-partisan office from Oscar Goodman, who served as mayor from 1999 to 2011.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DeSantis pushes back on 'delusional' Harris remark about Hurricane Milton calls
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) said Monday that it was “delusional” for Vice President Kamala Harris to accuse him of “political gamesmanship” after reports emerged that he did not take the Democratic presidential nominee’s calls as his state braces for Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court won’t hear Pennsylvania legislators' challenge to executive actions expanding voting access
item.description: The Supreme Court decided Monday it won’t take up a case to restore a lawsuit by Pennsylvania Republicans that challenged executive actions expanding voting access on the basis that only state legislatures can regulate federal elections.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: GOP senator calls Trump special counsel filing 'a temper tantrum from a deranged fanatic'
item.description: Arkansas Sen. Tom Cotton (R) said in a Sunday interview that a recently unsealed filing from special counsel Jack Smith is “a temper tantrum from a deranged fanatic.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota Republicans seek a reset after two years of DFL trifecta in St. Paul
item.description: All 134 Minnesota House seats are on the ballot this fall, along with one state Senate seat. The results could flip political control of the Legislature.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump's plan would add $4 trillion more to U.S. debt compared to Harris', analysis says
item.description: Neither former President Donald Trump nor Vice President Kamala Harris have plans to fix the national debt, risking "an eventual fiscal crisis," a budget nonprofit group found in its analysis.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett, MPR News
